items.headline: The Giants send struggling reliever Ryan Walker to the minors
items.description:
SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The San Francisco Giants sent struggling reliever Ryan Walker to Triple-A Sacramento on Sunday following his fourth straight outing when he allowed at least one run.
The move with Walker was one of several made before the Giants played a series finale against the Pittsburgh Pirates. Right-hander Gregory Santos was also optioned to Sacramento, while lefty Sam Hentges was activated from his rehabilitation assignment and righty Dylan Smith was called up from Triple-A.

items.headline: Justin Foscue rebounds from causing blooper with star SS Corey Seager with 1st homer for Rangers
items.description:
ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Justin Foscue finally went deep for the first time in the big leagues with the Texas Rangers. That home run created a much happier highlight than the embarrassing blooper-reel moment he knows he will see often in the future after running into their two-time World Series MVP shortstop.
“Getting your first home run is a big deal. ... And I almost took out the star shortstop,” Foscue said. “A lot of ups and downs.”

items.headline: This Date in Baseball - 39-year-old Pete Rose stole second base, third, and home in one inning
items.description:
May 11
1904 — Cy Young’s 23-inning no-hit string ended. The streak included two innings on April 25, six on April 30, a perfect game against the Philadelphia A’s on May 5, and six innings today.

items.headline: Twins right-hander Taj Bradley placed on IL with right pectoral inflammation
items.description:
CLEVELAND (AP) — The pause button has been pressed on Twins right-hander Taj Bradley’s comeback season.
Bradley was placed on the 15-day injured list Saturday with right pectoral inflammation, retroactive to Wednesday. He had an MRI in Minneapolis that revealed no significant damage and is slated to consult with Dr. Keith Meister.

items.headline: Braves batter Blake Snell for five runs in 7-2 win over Dodgers to end losing streak in Los Angeles
items.description:
LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ozzie Albies and Matt Olson each had two RBIs and the Atlanta Braves rocked Blake Snell for five runs in three innings in his season debut for a 7-2 win over the Los Angeles Dodgers on Saturday night.
Spencer Strider (1-0) threw six scoreless innings, Austin Riley, Michael Harris II and Drake Baldwin each drove in a run, and the Braves snapped an eight-game losing streak at Dodger Stadium.

items.headline: Rangers blank Cubs 6-0, snapping Chicago’s second 10-game win streak
items.description:
ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Josh Jung and Justin Foscue homered, Alejandro Osuna had a pair of RBI singles and the Texas Rangers beat the Cubs 6-0 on Saturday night, ending Chicago’s second 10-game winning streak of the season.
Jung, who finished with three hits, led off the second inning with a homer to snap an 0-for-13 skid since a 13-game hitting streak. Foscue’s first career homer leading off the fifth made it a 5-0 lead for the Rangers, who had lost five of six games.

items.headline: Ian Happ has reached base in 30 consecutive games for Cubs, and 2 others have double-digit streaks
items.description:
ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Cubs left fielder Ian Happ extended his on-base streak to 30 games with a first-inning walk in Chicago's 6-0 loss to Texas on Saturday night, and teammates Pete Crow-Armstrong and Michael Busch also kept up their double-digit streaks.
The only longer active streak in the majors is a 33-gamer by Nick Kurtz, who had two doubles Saturday for the Athletics in a 6-2 win over Baltimore.

items.headline: Phillies' Bryce Harper exits in the first inning vs. Rockies with a migraine
items.description:
PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Phillies first baseman Bryce Harper was removed in the bottom of the first inning against the Colorado Rockies Saturday night with what the team said was a migraine.
Harper, who has played in all 40 games this season, participated in fielding practice and played the top of the first. He fielded a grounder off the bat of Mickey Moniak for a putout.

items.headline: Giants ace Logan Webb goes on 15-day IL with right knee bursitis
items.description:
SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Giants starter Logan Webb has been put on the 15-day injured list Saturday because of right knee bursitis after struggling with knee discomfort in his last start, a 10-5 loss to the San Diego Padres.
San Francisco made the move retroactive to Wednesday.

items.headline: White Sox scratch Andrew Benintendi because of neck stiffness
items.description:
CHICAGO (AP) — White Sox outfielder Andrew Benintendi was scratched from Saturday night's lineup against Seattle because of neck stiffness.
Benintendi was slated to serve as the designated hitter. He was replaced by Randal Grichuk, who went 0 for 3 with a strikeout in a 6-1 victory over the Mariners.
